diagrams.title: TCP Header Structure
diagrams.description: Shows the structure of a TCP segment header with all fields and their bit positions
elements: Data Offset (4 bits) + Reserved (3 bits) + Flags (9 bits)
diagrams.purpose: Understanding TCP segment format and how reliability mechanisms are implemented

diagrams.title: Three-Way Handshake Process
diagrams.description: Step-by-step visualization of TCP connection establishment
elements: Server sends SYN-ACK with ack=x+1 and seq=y
diagrams.purpose: Visualizing how TCP synchronizes sequence numbers to establish reliable connection

diagrams.title: TCP State Transition Diagram (Connection States)
diagrams.description: Shows TCP connection states from LISTEN through ESTABLISHED to CLOSED
diagrams.purpose: Understanding TCP connection lifecycle and proper termination procedures

diagrams.title: Sliding Window Protocol
diagrams.description: Visual representation of TCP sliding window showing sent, unacknowledged, and allowed to send segments
elements: Window Size = 4 segments in this example
diagrams.purpose: Understanding how TCP manages multiple in-flight segments and flow control
